records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Said About Those Who Miss Standing at Al-mash'ar
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2992 - Muhammad ibn Hakim narrated:
I said to Abu Abdullah (as): "A blind man and a weak woman are traveling with an Arab camel driver. After departing from Arafat, he takes them directly to Mina without stopping at Jam‘ (Muzdalifah)."
Imam (as) said: "Did they not pray there (at Jam‘)? If so, it suffices for them."
I asked: "What if they did not pray there?"
Imam (as) replied: "If they remembered Allah (swt), the Almighty, while there, then it suffices for them."
records.arabicText: 2992 - وَ رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ حَكِيمٍ قَالَ : قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ اَلرَّجُلُ اَلْأَعْمَى وَ اَلْمَرْأَةُ اَلضَّعِيفَةُ يَكُونَانِ مَعَ اَلْجَمَّالِ اَلْأَعْرَابِيِّ فَإِذَا أَفَاضَ بِهِمْ مِنْ عَرَفَاتٍ مَرَّ بِهِمْ كَمَا هُمْ إِلَى مِنًى وَ لَمْ يَنْزِلْ بِهِمْ جَمْعاً فَقَالَ «أَ لَيْسَ قَدْ صَلَّوْا بِهَا فَقَدْ أَجْزَأَهُمْ» قُلْتُ فَإِنْ لَمْ يُصَلُّوا بِهَا قَالَ «ذَكَرُوا اَللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ فِيهَا فَإِنْ كَانُوا قَدْ ذَكَرُوا اَللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ فِيهَا فَقَدْ أَجْزَأَهُمْ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2992 - Muhammad ibn Hakim narrated:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
I said to Abu Abdullah (as): "A blind man and a weak woman are traveling with an Arab camel driver. After departing from Arafat, he takes them directly to Mina without stopping at Jam‘ (Muzdalifah)."
Imam (as) said: "Did they not pray there (at Jam‘)? If so, it suffices for them."
I asked: "What if they did not pray there?"
Imam (as) replied: "If they remembered Allah (swt), the Almighty, while there, then it suffices for them."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Those Permitted to Depart Early From Muzdalifah Before Fajr
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2993 - Ibn Muskan narrated from Abu Basir, who said: I heard Abu Abdullah (as) say:
"There is no harm in allowing women to depart earlier when the night has passed. They should stand at Al-Mash'ar Al-Haram for a brief moment, then proceed to Mina to throw the pebbles at the Jamarah. After that, they should wait briefly, then shorten their hair, and proceed to Mecca to perform tawaf. However, if they wish to have the sacrifice performed on their behalf, they should appoint someone to perform it for them."
records.arabicText: 2993 - رَوَى اِبْنُ مُسْكَانَ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ قَالَ سَمِعْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ يَقُولُ : «لاَ بَأْسَ بِأَنْ تُقَدَّمَ اَلنِّسَاءُ إِذَا زَالَ اَللَّيْلُ فَيَقِفْنَ عِنْدَ اَلْمَشْعَرِ اَلْحَرَامِ سَاعَةً ثُمَّ يُنْطَلَقُ بِهِنَّ إِلَى مِنًى فَيَرْمِينَ اَلْجَمْرَةَ ثُمَّ يَصْبِرْنَ سَاعَةً ثُمَّ يُقَصِّرْنَ وَ يُنْطَلَقُ بِهِنَّ إِلَى مَكَّةَ فَيَطُفْنَ إِلاَّ أَنْ يَكُنَّ يُرِدْنَ أَنْ يُذْبَحَ عَنْهُنَّ فَإِنَّهُنَّ يُوَكِّلْنَ مَنْ يَذْبَحُ عَنْهُنَّ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2993 - Ibn Muskan narrated from Abu Basir, who said: I heard Abu Abdullah (as) say:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "There is no harm in allowing women to depart earlier when the night has passed. They should stand at Al-Mash'ar Al-Haram for a brief moment, then proceed to Mina to throw the pebbles at the Jamarah. After that, they should wait briefly, then shorten their hair, and proceed to Mecca to perform tawaf. However, if they wish to have the sacrifice performed on their behalf, they should appoint someone to perform it for them."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Those Permitted to Depart Early From Muzdalifah Before Fajr
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2994 - Ali ibn Ri’ab narrated from Misma‘, from Abu Ibrahim (as), regarding a man who stood with the people at Jam‘ (Muzdalifah) and then departed before the people began to leave.
Imam (as) said: "If he was unaware, then there is nothing upon him. But if he departed before the break of dawn, then he must offer the sacrifice of a sheep."
records.arabicText: 2994 - وَ رَوَى عَلِيُّ بْنُ رِئَابٍ عَنْ مِسْمَعٍ عَنْ أَبِي إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي رَجُلٍ وَقَفَ مَعَ اَلنَّاسِ بِجَمْعٍ ثُمَّ أَفَاضَ قَبْلَ أَنْ يُفِيضَ اَلنَّاسُ قَالَ «إِنْ كَانَ جَاهِلاً فَلاَ شَيْءَ عَلَيْهِ وَ إِنْ كَانَ أَفَاضَ قَبْلَ طُلُوعِ اَلْفَجْرِ فَعَلَيْهِ دَمُ شَاةٍ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2994 - Ali ibn Ri’ab narrated from Misma‘, from Abu Ibrahim (as), regarding a man who stood with the people at Jam‘ (Muzdalifah) and then departed before the people began to leave.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "If he was unaware, then there is nothing upon him. But if he departed before the break of dawn, then he must offer the sacrifice of a sheep."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Said About Those Who Miss the Hajj
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2995 - Mu‘awiyah ibn Ammar narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
"Whoever reaches Jam‘ (Muzdalifah) has indeed attained the Hajj."
Imam (as) also said: "Any pilgrim performing Qiran (combining Hajj and Umrah), Ifrad (Hajj alone), or Tamattu‘ (Umrah followed by Hajj) who arrives after missing the Hajj should terminate their pilgrimage with an Umrah, and they are obligated to perform the Hajj in the following year."
Regarding a man who reaches the Imam while he is at Jam‘,
Imam (as) said: "If he believes he can reach Arafat and stand there briefly, and then return to Jam‘ before sunrise, he should go to Arafat. But if he believes he will not reach Arafat until after the people have left, then he should not proceed there, and his Hajj is complete."
records.arabicText: 2995 - رَوَى مُعَاوِيَةُ بْنُ عَمَّارٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «مَنْ أَدْرَكَ جَمْعاً فَقَدْ أَدْرَكَ اَلْحَجَّ » وَ قَالَ «أَيُّمَا قَارِنٍ أَوْ مُفْرِدٍ أَوْ مُتَمَتِّعٍ قَدِمَ وَ قَدْ فَاتَهُ اَلْحَجُّ فَلْيَحِلَّ بِعُمْرَةٍ وَ عَلَيْهِ اَلْحَجُّ مِنْ قَابِلٍ » قَالَ وَ قَالَ فِي رَجُلٍ أَدْرَكَ اَلْإِمَامَ وَ هُوَ بِجَمْعٍ فَقَالَ «إِنْ ظَنَّ أَنَّهُ يَأْتِي عَرَفَاتٍ فَيَقِفُ بِهَا قَلِيلاً ثُمَّ يُدْرِكُ جَمْعاً قَبْلَ طُلُوعِ اَلشَّمْسِ فَلْيَأْتِهَا فَإِنْ ظَنَّ أَنَّهُ لاَ يَأْتِيهَا حَتَّى يُفِيضُوا فَلاَ يَأْتِيهَا وَ قَدْ تَمَّ حَجُّهُ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2995 - Mu‘awiyah ibn Ammar narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Whoever reaches Jam‘ (Muzdalifah) has indeed attained the Hajj."
Imam (as) also said: "Any pilgrim performing Qiran (combining Hajj and Umrah), Ifrad (Hajj alone), or Tamattu‘ (Umrah followed by Hajj) who arrives after missing the Hajj should terminate their pilgrimage with an Umrah, and they are obligated to perform the Hajj in the following year."
Regarding a man who reaches the Imam while he is at Jam‘,
Imam (as) said: "If he believes he can reach Arafat and stand there briefly, and then return to Jam‘ before sunrise, he should go to Arafat. But if he believes he will not reach Arafat until after the people have left, then he should not proceed there, and his Hajj is complete."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Said About Those Who Miss the Hajj
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2996 - Ibn Mahbub narrated from Dawud al-Raqqi, who said:
"I was with Abu Abdullah (as) at Mina when a man came and said, 'There are people who arrived, but the Hajj has already passed them.'
Abu Abdullah (as) replied: 'We ask Allah (swt) for well-being. I think that each of them should slaughter a sheep, exit from their state of Ihram, and they are obligated to perform the Hajj in the following year if they return to their homelands. However, if they stay until the days of Tashreeq pass while in Mecca, then leave to the Miqat of the people of Mecca, enter Ihram from there, and perform an Umrah, then the obligation of performing the Hajj in the following year is lifted from them.'"
records.arabicText: 2996 - وَ رَوَى اِبْنُ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ دَاوُدَ اَلرَّقِّيِّ قَالَ : كُنْتُ مَعَ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ بِمِنًى إِذْ جَاءَ رَجُلٌ فَقَالَ إِنَّ قَوْماً قَدِمُوا وَ قَدْ فَاتَهُمُ اَلْحَجُّ فَقَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ «نَسْأَلُ اَللَّهَ اَلْعَافِيَةَ أَرَى أَنْ يُهَرِيقَ كُلُّ رَجُلٍ مِنْهُمْ شَاةً وَ يَحِلُّوا وَ عَلَيْهِمُ اَلْحَجُّ مِنْ قَابِلٍ إِنِ اِنْصَرَفُوا إِلَى بِلاَدِهِمْ وَ إِنْ أَقَامُوا حَتَّى تَمْضِيَ أَيَّامُ اَلتَّشْرِيقِ بِمَكَّةَ ثُمَّ خَرَجُوا إِلَى وَقْتِ أَهْلِ مَكَّةَ فَأَحْرَمُوا مِنْهُ وَ اِعْتَمَرُوا فَلَيْسَ عَلَيْهِمُ اَلْحَجُّ مِنْ قَابِلٍ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2996 - Ibn Mahbub narrated from Dawud al-Raqqi, who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"I was with Abu Abdullah (as) at Mina when a man came and said, 'There are people who arrived, but the Hajj has already passed them.'
Abu Abdullah (as) replied: 'We ask Allah (swt) for well-being. I think that each of them should slaughter a sheep, exit from their state of Ihram, and they are obligated to perform the Hajj in the following year if they return to their homelands. However, if they stay until the days of Tashreeq pass while in Mecca, then leave to the Miqat of the people of Mecca, enter Ihram from there, and perform an Umrah, then the obligation of performing the Hajj in the following year is lifted from them.'"

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Taking Pebbles for Jamarat From Inside or Outside the Sacred Area
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2997 - Hanan ibn Sadeer narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
"It is sufficient for you to take the pebbles for the Jamarat from anywhere within the boundaries of the Haram, except from the Sacred Mosque (Masjid al-Haram) and Masjid al-Khayf."
records.arabicText: 2997 - رَوَى حَنَانُ بْنُ سَدِيرٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «يُجْزِيكَ أَنْ تَأْخُذَ حَصَى اَلْجِمَارِ مِنَ اَلْحَرَمِ كُلِّهِ إِلاَّ مِنَ اَلْمَسْجِدِ اَلْحَرَامِ وَ مَسْجِدِ اَلْخَيْفِ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2997 - Hanan ibn Sadeer narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "It is sufficient for you to take the pebbles for the Jamarat from anywhere within the boundaries of the Haram, except from the Sacred Mosque (Masjid al-Haram) and Masjid al-Khayf."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Said About Those Who Fear Throwing the Pebbles or Who Add or Reduce the Number
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2998 - Ali ibn Abi Hamzah narrated from Abu Basir, who said:
"I said to Abu Abdullah (as), 'I went to perform the stoning (Rami), and I found six pebbles in my hand.' He said, 'Take one from beneath your feet.'"
records.arabicText: 2998 - رَوَى عَلِيُّ بْنُ أَبِي حَمْزَةَ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ قَالَ : قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ ذَهَبْتُ أَرْمِي فَإِذَا فِي يَدِي سِتُّ حَصَيَاتٍ فَقَالَ «خُذْ وَاحِدَةً مِنْ تَحْتِ رِجْلَيْكَ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2998 - Ali ibn Abi Hamzah narrated from Abu Basir, who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "I said to Abu Abdullah (as), 'I went to perform the stoning (Rami), and I found six pebbles in my hand.' He said, 'Take one from beneath your feet.'"

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Said About Those Who Fear Throwing the Pebbles or Who Add or Reduce the Number
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2999 - And in another narration:
"Do not take the pebbles for stoning (Rami) from those that have already been used."
records.arabicText: 2999 - وَ فِي خَبَرٍ آخَرَ : «وَ لاَ تَأْخُذْ مِنْ حَصَى اَلْجِمَارِ اَلَّذِي قَدْ رُمِيَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2999 - And in another narration:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Do not take the pebbles for stoning (Rami) from those that have already been used."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Said About Those Who Fear Throwing the Pebbles or Who Add or Reduce the Number
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3000 - Mu'awiya ibn Ammar narrated from Abu Abdullah (as):
Regarding a man who took 21 pebbles for stoning (Rami) and threw them, realizing afterward that he had one extra but could not determine which station he had missed: "He should return and throw one pebble at each station. If a pebble falls from someone's hand and they do not know which one it is, they should take one from under their feet and throw it."
Imam (as) also said: "If you throw a pebble and it lands in a litter (carrying device), you should replace it with another. However, if it hits a person or an animal and then lands on the target (the pile of pebbles), it is sufficient."
Regarding someone who threw only four pebbles at the first station but threw seven at the second and third: "He should return and throw three more at the first station, and the rest is complete. If he threw only three at the second station and completed the third, he should return and throw seven at the second. If he threw four at the second station, he must return and throw three more."
When asked about someone who performs the stoning in reverse order,
Imam (as) replied: "He must redo the stoning for the middle station and the Jamrat al-Aqaba in order."
records.arabicText: 3000 - وَ رَوَى مُعَاوِيَةُ بْنُ عَمَّارٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي رَجُلٍ أَخَذَ إِحْدَى وَ عِشْرِينَ حَصَاةً فَرَمَى بِهَا وَ زَادَتْ وَاحِدَةٌ وَ لَمْ يَدْرِ أَيُّهُنَّ نَقَصَتْ قَالَ «فَلْيَرْجِعْ فَلْيَرْمِ كُلَّ وَاحِدَةٍ بِحَصَاةٍ وَ إِنْ سَقَطَتْ مِنْ رَجُلٍ حَصَاةٌ وَ لَمْ يَدْرِ أَيَّتُهُنَّ هِيَ فَلْيَأْخُذْ مِنْ تَحْتِ قَدَمَيْهِ حَصَاةً فَيَرْمِي بِهَا» قَالَ «فَإِنْ رَمَيْتَ بِحَصَاةٍ فَوَقَعَتْ فِي مَحْمِلٍ فَأَعِدْ مَكَانَهَا وَ إِنْ أَصَابَتْ إِنْسَاناً أَوْ جَمَلاً ثُمَّ وَقَعَتْ عَلَى اَلْجِمَارِ أَجْزَأَكَ » وَ قَالَ فِي رَجُلٍ رَمَى اَلْجِمَارَ فَرَمَى اَلْأُولَى بِأَرْبَعِ حَصَيَاتٍ ثُمَّ رَمَى اَلْأَخِيرَتَيْنِ بِسَبْعٍ سَبْعٍ قَالَ «يَعُودُ فَيَرْمِي اَلْأُولَى بِثَلاَثٍ وَ قَدْ فَرَغَ وَ إِنْ كَانَ رَمَى اَلْوُسْطَى بِثَلاَثٍ ثُمَّ رَمَى اَلْأُخْرَى فَلْيَرْمِ اَلْوُسْطَى بِسَبْعٍ وَ إِنْ كَانَ رَمَى اَلْوُسْطَى بِأَرْبَعٍ رَجَعَ فَرَمَى بِثَلاَثٍ» قَالَ قُلْتُ اَلرَّجُلُ يَرْمِي اَلْجِمَارَ مَنْكُوسَةً قَالَ «يُعِيدُهَا عَلَى اَلْوُسْطَى وَ جَمْرَةِ اَلْعَقَبَةِ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3000 - Mu'awiya ibn Ammar narrated from Abu Abdullah (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn:
Regarding a man who took 21 pebbles for stoning (Rami) and threw them, realizing afterward that he had one extra but could not determine which station he had missed: "He should return and throw one pebble at each station. If a pebble falls from someone's hand and they do not know which one it is, they should take one from under their feet and throw it."
Imam (as) also said: "If you throw a pebble and it lands in a litter (carrying device), you should replace it with another. However, if it hits a person or an animal and then lands on the target (the pile of pebbles), it is sufficient."
Regarding someone who threw only four pebbles at the first station but threw seven at the second and third: "He should return and throw three more at the first station, and the rest is complete. If he threw only three at the second station and completed the third, he should return and throw seven at the second. If he threw four at the second station, he must return and throw three more."
When asked about someone who performs the stoning in reverse order,
Imam (as) replied: "He must redo the stoning for the middle station and the Jamrat al-Aqaba in order."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Said About Those Who Fear Throwing the Pebbles or Who Add or Reduce the Number
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3001 - Muhammad ibn Muslim narrated from Abu Abdullah (as) that he said:
"For one who is fearful, there is no harm in stoning the Jamarat at night, slaughtering the sacrifice at night, and performing the Tawaf of Ifadah at night."
records.arabicText: 3001 - وَ رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ : «فِي اَلْخَائِفِ لاَ بَأْسَ بِأَنْ يَرْمِيَ اَلْجِمَارَ بِاللَّيْلِ وَ يُضَحِّيَ بِاللَّيْلِ وَ يُفِيضَ بِاللَّيْلِ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3001 - Muhammad ibn Muslim narrated from Abu Abdullah (as) that he said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "For one who is fearful, there is no harm in stoning the Jamarat at night, slaughtering the sacrifice at night, and performing the Tawaf of Ifadah at night."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Said About Those Who Fear Throwing the Pebbles or Who Add or Reduce the Number
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3002 - Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a woman who, out of ignorance, did not stone the Jamarat before departing to Mecca.
Imam (as) replied: "She should return and stone the Jamarat as she would have originally done. The same applies to a man in such a case."
records.arabicText: 3002 - وَ سَأَلَهُ مُعَاوِيَةُ بْنُ عَمَّارٍ : عَنِ اِمْرَأَةٍ جَهِلَتْ أَنْ تَرْمِيَ اَلْجِمَارَ حَتَّى نَفَرَتْ إِلَى مَكَّةَ قَالَ «فَلْتَرْجِعْ فَتَرْمِي اَلْجِمَارَ كَمَا كَانَتْ تَرْمِي وَ اَلرَّجُلُ كَذَلِكَ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3002 - Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a woman who, out of ignorance, did not stone the Jamarat before departing to Mecca.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) replied: "She should return and stone the Jamarat as she would have originally done. The same applies to a man in such a case."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Said About Those Who Fear Throwing the Pebbles or Who Add or Reduce the Number
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3003 - Abdullah ibn Sinan narrated from Imam (as) regarding a man who left Muzdalifah (Jam') for Mina but encountered an issue and did not stone the Jamarat until the sun had set.
Imam (as) said: "He should perform the stoning twice the next morning: once early in the morning for the previous day and again at the time of noon for the current day."
records.arabicText: 3003 - وَ رَوَى عَنْهُ عَبْدُ اَللَّهِ بْنُ سِنَانٍ : فِي رَجُلٍ أَفَاضَ مِنْ جَمْعٍ حَتَّى اِنْتَهَى إِلَى مِنًى فَعَرَضَ لَهُ شَيْءٌ فَلَمْ يَرْمِ اَلْجَمْرَةَ حَتَّى غَابَتِ اَلشَّمْسُ قَالَ «يَرْمِي إِذَا أَصْبَحَ مَرَّتَيْنِ إِحْدَاهُمَا بُكْرَةً وَ هِيَ لِلْأَمْسِ وَ اَلْأُخْرَى عِنْدَ زَوَالِ اَلشَّمْسِ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.3003 - Abdullah ibn Sinan narrated from Imam (as) regarding a man who left Muzdalifah (Jam') for Mina but encountered an issue and did not stone the Jamarat until the sun had set.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "He should perform the stoning twice the next morning: once early in the morning for the previous day and again at the time of noon for the current day."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Those Permitted to Throw Pebbles at Night
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3004 - Waheeb ibn Hafs narrated from Abu Basir, who said: I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about those who are permitted to stone the Jamarat at night.
Imam (as) said: "The wood-gatherer, the servant who has no control over his own affairs, the fearful, the debtor, and the sick person who cannot stone. He should be carried to the Jamarat, and if he is able to stone, he should do so; otherwise, someone should stone on his behalf while he is present."
records.arabicText: 3004 - رَوَى وُهَيْبُ بْنُ حَفْصٍ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنِ اَلَّذِي يَنْبَغِي لَهُ أَنْ يَرْمِيَ بِاللَّيْلِ مَنْ هُوَ قَالَ «اَلْحَاطِبَةُ وَ اَلْمَمْلُوكُ اَلَّذِي لاَ يَمْلِكُ مِنْ أَمْرِهِ شَيْئاً وَ اَلْخَائِفُ وَ اَلْمَدِينُ وَ اَلْمَرِيضُ اَلَّذِي لاَ يَسْتَطِيعُ أَنْ يَرْمِيَ يُحْمَلُ إِلَى اَلْجِمَارِ فَإِنْ قَدَرَ عَلَى أَنْ يَرْمِيَ وَ إِلاَّ فَارْمِ عَنْهُ وَ هُوَ حَاضِرٌ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.3004 - Waheeb ibn Hafs narrated from Abu Basir, who said: I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about those who are permitted to stone the Jamarat at night.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The wood-gatherer, the servant who has no control over his own affairs, the fearful, the debtor, and the sick person who cannot stone. He should be carried to the Jamarat, and if he is able to stone, he should do so; otherwise, someone should stone on his behalf while he is present."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Throwing Pebbles on Behalf of the Sick and Children
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3005 - Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar and Abdur-Rahman ibn Al-Hajjaj narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
"The injured and those suffering from abdominal illnesses should have the Jamarat stoned on their behalf, and likewise for the children, the stoning should be done on their behalf."
records.arabicText: 3005 - رَوَى مُعَاوِيَةُ بْنُ عَمَّارٍ وَ عَبْدُ اَلرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ اَلْحَجَّاجِ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «اَلْكَسِيرُ وَ اَلْمَبْطُونُ يُرْمَى عَنْهُمَا قَالَ وَ اَلصِّبْيَانُ يُرْمَى عَنْهُمْ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3005 - Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar and Abdur-Rahman ibn Al-Hajjaj narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The injured and those suffering from abdominal illnesses should have the Jamarat stoned on their behalf, and likewise for the children, the stoning should be done on their behalf."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Throwing Pebbles on Behalf of the Sick and Children
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3006 - Ishaq ibn Ammar asked Abu Al-Hasan Musa (as) about a sick person: Can the stoning of the Jamarat be performed on his behalf?
Imam (as) replied: "Yes, he should be carried to the Jamarat, and the stoning should be done on his behalf."
I said: "What if he is unable to be carried?"
Imam (as) replied: "He may remain in his residence, and the stoning should be performed on his behalf."
records.arabicText: 3006 - وَ سَأَلَ إِسْحَاقُ بْنُ عَمَّارٍ أَبَا اَلْحَسَنِ مُوسَى عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : عَنِ اَلْمَرِيضِ يُرْمَى عَنْهُ اَلْجِمَارُ قَالَ «نَعَمْ يُحْمَلْ إِلَى اَلْجَمْرَةِ وَ يُرْمَى عَنْهُ» قُلْتُ لاَ يُطِيقُ ذَلِكَ فَقَالَ «يُتْرَكُ فِي مَنْزِلِهِ وَ يُرْمَى عَنْهُ ».

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Those Who Spend the Nights of Mina in Mecca
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3007 - Ibn Maskan narrated from Ja’far ibn Najiyah, from Abu Abdullah (as):
I asked him about someone who spends the nights of Mina in Mecca.
Imam (as) replied: "He must offer three sheep as a sacrifice."
records.arabicText: 3007 - رَوَى اِبْنُ مُسْكَانَ عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ نَاجِيَةَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُهُ عَمَّنْ بَاتَ لَيَالِيَ مِنًى بِمَكَّةَ فَقَالَ «عَلَيْهِ ثَلاَثَةٌ مِنَ اَلْغَنَمِ يَذْبَحُهُنَّ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3007 - Ibn Maskan narrated from Ja’far ibn Najiyah, from Abu Abdullah (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn:
I asked him about someone who spends the nights of Mina in Mecca.
Imam (as) replied: "He must offer three sheep as a sacrifice."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Those Who Spend the Nights of Mina in Mecca
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3008 - Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a man who visited the House (Kaaba) and remained engaged in his circumambulation, supplications, and Sa'i (ritual walking) until dawn broke.
Imam (as) replied: "There is nothing upon him; he was engaged in the obedience of Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic."
records.arabicText: 3008 - وَ سَأَلَهُ مُعَاوِيَةُ بْنُ عَمَّارٍ : عَنْ رَجُلٍ زَارَ اَلْبَيْتَ فَلَمْ يَزَلْ فِي طَوَافِهِ وَ دُعَائِهِ وَ اَلسَّعْيِ وَ اَلدُّعَاءِ حَتَّى طَلَعَ اَلْفَجْرُ قَالَ «لَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ شَيْءٌ كَانَ فِي طَاعَةِ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.3008 - Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a man who visited the House (Kaaba) and remained engaged in his circumambulation, supplications, and Sa'i (ritual walking) until dawn broke.
Imam (as) replied:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "There is nothing upon him; he was engaged in the obedience of Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Those Who Spend the Nights of Mina in Mecca
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3009 - Jamil ibn Darraj narrated from Abu Abdullah (as) that he said:
"If you leave Mina before sunset, then do not let the morning come upon you except while you are still in Mina."
records.arabicText: 3009 - وَ رَوَى عَنْهُ جَمِيلُ بْنُ دَرَّاجٍ أَنَّهُ قَالَ : «إِذَا خَرَجْتَ مِنْ مِنًى قَبْلَ غُرُوبِ اَلشَّمْسِ فَلاَ تُصْبِحْ إِلاَّ بِهَا».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3009 - Jamil ibn Darraj narrated from Abu Abdullah (as) that he said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If you leave Mina before sunset, then do not let the morning come upon you except while you are still in Mina."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Those Who Spend the Nights of Mina in Mecca
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3010 - Ja'far ibn Najiya narrated from Abu Abdullah (as) that he said:
"If a man leaves Mina at the beginning of the night, then the night should not reach its midpoint except while he is still in Mina. However, if he leaves after midnight, then it is permissible for him to spend the morning elsewhere."
records.arabicText: 3010 - وَ رَوَى عَنْهُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ جَعْفَرُ بْنُ نَاجِيَةَ أَنَّهُ قَالَ : «إِذَا خَرَجَ اَلرَّجُلُ مِنْ مِنًى أَوَّلَ اَللَّيْلِ فَلاَ يَنْتَصِفْ لَهُ اَللَّيْلُ إِلاَّ وَ هُوَ بِمِنًى وَ إِذَا خَرَجَ بَعْدَ نِصْفِ اَللَّيْلِ فَلاَ بَأْسَ أَنْ يُصْبِحَ بِغَيْرِهَا».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3010 - Ja'far ibn Najiya narrated from Abu Abdullah (as) that he said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If a man leaves Mina at the beginning of the night, then the night should not reach its midpoint except while he is still in Mina. However, if he leaves after midnight, then it is permissible for him to spend the morning elsewhere."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Those Who Spend the Nights of Mina in Mecca
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3011 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"Do not enter your homes in Mecca when you have visited, meaning the residents of Mecca."
records.arabicText: 3011 - وَ قَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «لاَ تَدْخُلُوا مَنَازِلَكُمْ بِمَكَّةَ إِذَا زُرْتُمْ يَعْنِي أَهْلَ مَكَّةَ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3011 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Do not enter your homes in Mecca when you have visited, meaning the residents of Mecca."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Those Who Spend the Nights of Mina in Mecca
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3012 - Ibn Abi Umayr narrated from Hisham ibn Al-Hakam, from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
"If a pilgrim visits from Mina, leaves Mecca, passes by the houses of Mecca, sleeps, and then wakes up before returning to Mina, there is no obligation upon him."
records.arabicText: 3012 - وَ رَوَى اِبْنُ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ عَنْ هِشَامِ بْنِ اَلْحَكَمِ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «إِذَا زَارَ اَلْحَاجُّ مِنْ مِنًى فَخَرَجَ مِنْ مَكَّةَ فَجَازَ بُيُوتَ مَكَّةَ فَنَامَ ثُمَّ أَصْبَحَ قَبْلَ أَنْ يَأْتِيَ مِنًى فَلاَ شَيْءَ عَلَيْهِ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3012 - Ibn Abi Umayr narrated from Hisham ibn Al-Hakam, from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If a pilgrim visits from Mina, leaves Mecca, passes by the houses of Mecca, sleeps, and then wakes up before returning to Mina, there is no obligation upon him."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Returning to Mecca After the Visit for Tawaf
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3013 - Jamil narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
"There is no harm if a man goes to Mecca, performs Tawaf during the days of Mina, and does not stay overnight there."
records.arabicText: 3013 - رَوَى جَمِيلٌ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «لاَ بَأْسَ أَنْ يَأْتِيَ اَلرَّجُلُ مَكَّةَ فَيَطُوفَ أَيَّامَ مِنًى وَ لاَ يَبِيتَ بِهَا».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3013 - Jamil narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "There is no harm if a man goes to Mecca, performs Tawaf during the days of Mina, and does not stay overnight there."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Returning to Mecca After the Visit for Tawaf
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3014 - Laith Al-Muradi asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a man who goes to Mecca during the days of Mina after completing the visitation of the House and performs Tawaf around the Kaaba voluntarily.
Imam (as) replied: "Staying in Mina is more beloved to me."
records.arabicText: 3014 - وَ سَأَلَهُ لَيْثٌ اَلْمُرَادِيُّ : عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يَأْتِي مَكَّةَ أَيَّامَ مِنًى بَعْدَ فَرَاغِهِ مِنْ زِيَارَةِ اَلْبَيْتِ فَيَطُوفُ بِالْبَيْتِ تَطَوُّعاً فَقَالَ «اَلْمُقَامُ بِمِنًى أَحَبُّ إِلَيَّ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.3014 - Laith Al-Muradi asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a man who goes to Mecca during the days of Mina after completing the visitation of the House and performs Tawaf around the Kaaba voluntarily.
Imam (as) replied:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Staying in Mina is more beloved to me."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the First and Final Departure From Mina
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3015 - Muawiya ibn Ammar narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
"If you intend to leave on the second day (of Tashreeq), it is not permissible for you to depart until the sun has passed its zenith. However, if you delay until the last day of Tashreeq, which is the final day of departure, then there is no issue with leaving at any time, whether you throw the pebbles before or after the zenith."
records.arabicText: 3015 - رَوَى مُعَاوِيَةُ بْنُ عَمَّارٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «إِذَا أَرَدْتَ أَنْ تَنْفِرَ فِي يَوْمَيْنِ فَلَيْسَ لَكَ أَنْ تَنْفِرَ حَتَّى تَزُولَ اَلشَّمْسُ فَإِنْ تَأَخَّرْتَ إِلَى آخِرِ أَيَّامِ اَلتَّشْرِيقِ وَ هُوَ يَوْمُ اَلنَّفْرِ اَلْأَخِيرِ فَلاَ عَلَيْكَ أَيَّ سَاعَةٍ نَفَرْتَ وَ رَمَيْتَ قَبْلَ اَلزَّوَالِ أَوْ بَعْدَهُ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3015 - Muawiya ibn Ammar narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If you intend to leave on the second day (of Tashreeq), it is not permissible for you to depart until the sun has passed its zenith. However, if you delay until the last day of Tashreeq, which is the final day of departure, then there is no issue with leaving at any time, whether you throw the pebbles before or after the zenith."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the First and Final Departure From Mina
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3016 - He said, and I heard him say regarding the verse of Allah (swt), the Exalted:
"Whoever hastens to leave in two days, there is no sin upon him, and whoever delays, there is no sin upon him—for the one who is conscious (of Allah (swt))." (Surah Al-Baqarah 2:203):
"It means that one should avoid hunting until the people of Mina depart on the final day of departure (nafr al-akhir)."
records.arabicText: 3016 - قَالَ وَ سَمِعْتُهُ يَقُولُ : فِي قَوْلِ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ: «۝ فَمَنْ تَعَجَّلَ فِي يَوْمَيْنِ فَلاٰ إِثْمَ عَلَيْهِ وَ مَنْ تَأَخَّرَ فَلاٰ إِثْمَ عَلَيْهِ لِمَنِ اِتَّقىٰ ۝» فَقَالَ «يَتَّقِي اَلصَّيْدَ حَتَّى يَنْفِرَ أَهْلُ مِنًى فِي اَلنَّفْرِ اَلْأَخِيرِ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.3016 - He said, and I heard him say regarding the verse of Allah (swt), the Exalted:
"Whoever hastens to leave in two days, there is no sin upon him, and whoever delays, there is no sin upon him—for the one who is conscious (of Allah (swt))." (Surah Al-Baqarah 2:203):
records.thaqalaynMatn: "It means that one should avoid hunting until the people of Mina depart on the final day of departure (nafr al-akhir)."
